inputQuestion: Build a scientific stock-selection playbook for 1-week to 1-month holds on NIFTY 50 with high win-rate, proper risk management and position sizing, requiring only weekly analysis effort.

researchQuery.statement: Evidence-based, low-frequency stock-selection strategies (1-week to 4-week holding periods, weekly rebalancing) with statistically significant positive expectancy, realistic win-rate ranges, and empirically supported position-sizing/stop-loss frameworks.
exclusions: pure ML/black-box methods without economic mechanism
claims of >75% win-rate without expectancy and out-of-sample testing

inferredFilters.search: [multi-pass: cross-sectional momentum; 52-week high momentum; post earnings announcement drift; Kelly criterion optimal portfolio sizing; volatility targeting time series momentum; stop-loss strategy momentum drawdown; low volatility anomaly betting against beta quality minus junk; momentum crashes drawdown bear market reversal]

shortlist.title: On Persistence in Mutual Fund Performance
Investor Psychology and Security Market Under- and Overreactions
The Adjustment of Stock Prices to New Information (FFJR-style)
Editor's Choice: Digesting Anomalies — An Investment Approach
… and the Cross-Section of Expected Returns
Investor Inattention and Friday Earnings Announcements
Post-Earnings-Announcement Drift: Delayed Price Response or Risk Premium?
Driven to Distraction: Extraneous Events and Underreaction to Earnings News
Analyzing the Analysts: When Do Recommendations Add Value?
Understanding the Nature of the Risks and the Source of the Rewards to Momentum Investing (Grundy & Martin)
Is There a Replication Crisis in Finance?
Risk Everywhere: Modeling and Managing Volatility (Bollerslev-Hood-Huss-Pedersen)
Pseudo-Mathematics and Financial Charlatanism: Backtest Overfitting on OOS Performance
Reducing Sequence Risk Using Trend Following and the CAPE Ratio (Faber-Richardson)

claims.text: Cross-sectional momentum (12-1m) earns ~0.8-1.0%/month winners-minus-losers spread, replicated across 40+ international markets and 217 years.
claims.evidence: Carhart 1997 momentum factor; Grundy-Martin 2001 decomposition; Baltussen et al. 2021 217-year out-of-sample.

claims.text: Momentum returns conditional on prior 36-month market state: +0.93%/month after up-markets, -0.37%/month after down-markets; large negative tails after bear bottoms.
claims.evidence: Cooper-Gutierrez-Hameed 2004 explicit conditional-mean test on CRSP 1929-1995.

claims.text: PEAD: top-decile standardised earnings surprise outperforms bottom by ~1%/month for 60 trading days post-announcement; effect is amplified for Friday announcements and high-distraction days.
claims.evidence: Bernard-Thomas 1989 original; DellaVigna-Pollet 2009 Friday effect; Hirshleifer-Lim-Teoh 2009 distraction effect.

claims.text: Quality (profitability + safety + growth) earns 3-6%/year alpha vs market across 24 countries.
Volatility scaling (inverse to realised vol, target portfolio vol 10-12%) raises Sharpe ratios 30-50% by avoiding crash periods.
claims.evidence: Moreira-Muir 2017 JF; Bollerslev et al. 2018 'Risk Everywhere'.

claims.text: Quarterly change in consensus analyst recommendation/EPS predicts forward 1-3 month returns; the level does not.
~65% of 452 published anomalies fail to replicate after correcting for microcaps and t-stat>3 hurdle. Survivors: momentum, profitability/quality, investment, low-vol/BAB.
claims.evidence: Hou-Xue-Zhang 2023 replication study; Harvey-Liu-Zhu 2015 t-stat 3.0 hurdle.

claims.text: With 7 backtest trials, expected max in-sample Sharpe of a true-zero-Sharpe strategy is ~1.0; with 50 trials, ~1.6.
claims.evidence: Bailey-Borwein-Lopez de Prado-Zhu 2014 analytical derivation + simulation.

claims.text: Holding equities only when index price > 10-month MA reduces max drawdown from ~50% to ~20% with similar long-run CAGR.

hypotheses.text: Weekly-rebalanced equal-risk-weighted portfolio of top-quintile NIFTY 50 stocks ranked by 12-1m momentum, gated by NIFTY > 200-DMA, vol-targeted at 12% portfolio vol with 7% individual stop, will produce 1-month win-rate 55-62% and gross monthly expected return 0.8-1.5% with max monthly drawdown -5% to -8%.
Adding PEAD overlay (top 5 NIFTY 50 by post-earnings standardised surprise from prior week) increases monthly Sharpe by 0.2-0.4 vs pure momentum.
Quality screen (ROE>15%, NetDebt/EBITDA<2, low EPS volatility) before momentum ranking improves Sharpe by 0.1-0.3 with similar drawdowns.
Analyst-revision momentum overlay outperforms price-momentum in 3 months after major macro shocks because price momentum is reset but revisions adapt with a lag.
Investors who self-discover indicators on NIFTY 50 daily data have >90% probability of producing strategies with true forward Sharpe <0.5 despite backtested Sharpe >1.5.

contradictions.summary: Fama 1997 argues most anomalies are methodological artefacts; Carhart 1997, Asness 2018, Baltussen 2021 show momentum and quality robust globally and over 200+ years. Resolution: premium exists but is smaller than originally reported and depends on careful implementation.
Cooper-Gutierrez-Hameed showed momentum failure after down-markets in U.S. 1929-1995; Indian replication is sparse, especially around the conditional-mean decomposition.

Academic 'win rate' is usually monthly portfolio level (% positive months) or trade-level (% positive trades). 55-62% applies to trade-level; portfolio monthly win-rate is 60-72% due to diversification. The user's 75% framing conflates the two.
Hou-Xue-Zhang argue investment+ROE explain momentum; Carhart treats momentum as independent. Practical resolution: joint use of momentum AND quality is well-supported regardless.

scores.notes: Most claims rest on multiple top-tier journal papers with t-stats>3, multi-decade and multi-country evidence. Hypotheses H1-H3 are explicitly falsifiable with concrete numeric ranges. Indian-market replication of C2 is the main gap.

structuralInterventions.failureMode: C10 (stop-loss effectiveness) is abstract-level only; no in-shortlist quantification for Indian large-caps
structuralInterventions.structuralChange: Run a follow-up retrieval pass on Han-Yang-Zhou 2013 and Indian-market momentum stop-loss replications to harden C10.

structuralInterventions.failureMode: Playbook is grounded in U.S./global evidence; Indian-market replication is partial
structuralInterventions.structuralChange: Backtest H1 explicitly on NIFTY 50 2010-2025 with 2010-2018 in-sample and 2019-2025 OOS. Compute monthly distribution, max drawdown, Calmar, deflated Sharpe (Lopez de Prado correction).

structuralInterventions.failureMode: User's '75% accuracy' mental model will undermine sticking with the system through normal 4-loss streaks
structuralInterventions.structuralChange: Pre-commit by printing expected loss-streak distribution at the start (e.g., at 60% win rate, expect 4-loss streak ~5x/year) so user does not abandon system after one bad week.

structuralInterventions.failureMode: Transaction costs for Indian retail not in literature
structuralInterventions.structuralChange: Build an explicit cost model: STT 0.1% sell-side; SEBI/exchange 0.005%; slippage 0.05-0.10% per side; round-trip ~0.20-0.30%. Subtract from per-trade gross.

structuralInterventions.failureMode: Concentration risk when sector momentum is uniform (all financials)
structuralInterventions.structuralChange: Add a max-3-stocks-per-sector cap to final selection.

refinementNotes.pass1: Dropped 9 cross-domain papers from 4 search passes (HFT-arms-race, regulation, COVID, ESG, blockchain, marketing co-creation, currency-policy, IPO antitakeover). 21 in-domain papers surfaced; 19 retained as primary citations. Pass D required a retry with different keywords because initial query drifted to financial-regulation papers.
refinementNotes.pass2: Strengthened Section 0 'blunt truth' in response to the user's '75% accuracy' framing. Tightened H4 to speculative because analyst-revision-after-shock effect not directly cited in shortlist. Added anti-overfit guardrails (Section 8.5) after re-reading W19. Added explicit cost model in Sections 7 and 8.4 because no academic paper makes Indian execution costs concrete.

finalSummary: Seven literature-supported anomalies (12-1m momentum, PEAD, quality, low-vol/BAB, analyst revision, market-state filter, vol targeting) combine into a weekly-rebalanced playbook on the user's NIFTY 50 universe. Realistic monthly net return: 0.6-1.1% with worst-month drawdown -4% to -8%. Trade-level win-rate 52-62%, NOT >75%. Anti-overfit guardrails are the single most important section because the user's prior 5-6 year ML history is a high-risk base rate. MongoDB persistence pending MCP recovery; full markdown deliverable persisted to docs/research-runs/.
